question:
I'm new to this codebase and need a real code-based tour before my first behavior change.

Find the central runtime flow, trace it from the public API to the code that does the work, and show the nearby code paths and tests I should read next.

shellCommands: "C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"\\b(block_on|spawn|Runtime::new|Handle::block_on|Builder::new_current_thread|Builder::new_multi_thread)\\b\" tokio\\tests\\rt_basic.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_handle_block_on.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_spawn_blocking.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_threaded.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_current_thread.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_multi_thread.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_handle.rs tokio\\tests\\task_builder.rs tokio\\src\\runtime\\tests\\mod.rs tokio\\src\\runtime\\tests\\task.rs tokio\\src\\runtime\\tests\\task_combinations.rs"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"pub fn new|pub fn build|pub fn block_on|pub fn spawn|pub fn current\\(|pub fn try_current\\(\" tokio\\src\\runtime\\runtime.rs tokio\\src\\runtime\\builder.rs tokio\\src\\runtime\\handle.rs tokio\\src\\task\\spawn.rs tokio\\src\\lib.rs"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"fn .*\" tokio\\tests\\rt_basic.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_handle_block_on.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_spawn_blocking.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_threaded.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_current_thread.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_multi_thread.rs tokio\\tests\\rt_handle.rs tokio\\tests\\task_builder.rs"
"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"pub use task::spawn|pub use runtime::|pub mod runtime|mod runtime\" tokio\\src\\lib.rs tokio\\src\\runtime\\mod.rs"

"C:\\Windows\\System32\\WindowsPowerShell\\v1.0\\powershell.exe" -Command "rg -n \"pub\\(crate\\) fn enter_runtime|struct EnterRuntime|fn enter_runtime|pub\\(super\\) fn enter_runtime\" tokio\\src\\runtime\\context.rs tokio\\src\\runtime\\context\\runtime.rs tokio\\src\\runtime\\context\\current.rs tokio\\src\\runtime\\context\\runtime_mt.rs"
graph.answer:
The runtime center is:

`#[tokio::main]` / `Runtime::new` / `tokio::spawn` -> `Builder` -> `Runtime`/`Handle` -> scheduler-specific `spawn`/`block_on`

Here’s the concrete path.

**Runtime Flow**
- `#[tokio::main]` is the highest-level public entrypoint. The macro expands to runtime-builder code and a `block_on` call; the docs in [`tokio-macros/src/lib.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio-macros/src/lib.rs#L99) show the equivalent `Builder::new_*().enable_all().build().unwrap().block_on(...)`, and the implementation lives in [`tokio-macros/src/entry.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio-macros/src/entry.rs#L582).
- The runtime public API is re-exported from [`tokio/src/runtime/mod.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/mod.rs#L616), and `tokio::spawn` is re-exported from [`tokio/src/lib.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/lib.rs#L577).
- `Runtime::new` is just a convenience constructor: [`tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L174) calls `Builder::new_multi_thread().enable_all().build()`.
- `Builder::build` decides which scheduler to construct in [`tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs#L1072). The actual assembly happens in:
  - current-thread: [`build_current_thread_runtime`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs#L1646)
  - multi-thread: [`build_threaded_runtime`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/builder.rs#L2003)
- `Runtime::block_on` is the main driver entrypoint in [`tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L340). It enters runtime context and then dispatches to the chosen scheduler’s `block_on`.
- `Handle::block_on` exists too in [`tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs#L342), but on current-thread runtimes it does not drive I/O/timer the way `Runtime::block_on` does.

**Spawn Path**
- `tokio::spawn` is implemented in [`tokio/src/task/spawn.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/task/spawn.rs#L174).
- Its `spawn_inner` path looks up the active runtime through [`runtime/context/current.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context/current.rs#L33), then calls the runtime handle.
- `runtime::Handle::spawn` in [`tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs#L197) forwards into the internal scheduler handle.
- The task is actually bound and queued in the scheduler-specific code:
  - current-thread: [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs#L495) and its `Schedule` impl at [`#L702`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs#L702)
  - multi-thread: [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/handle.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/handle.rs#L55), with bind/schedule at [`#L81`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/handle.rs#L81) and [`#L110`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/handle.rs#L110)
- The runtime-context guard that makes `spawn` and `block_on` legal lives in [`tokio/src/runtime/context/runtime.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context/runtime.rs#L35).

**Block-On Path**
- `Runtime::block_on` enters runtime context, then calls the scheduler:
  - current-thread scheduler: [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs#L203)
  - multi-thread scheduler: [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/mod.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/mod.rs#L87)
- The runtime-context plumbing is in [`tokio/src/runtime/context/runtime.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context/runtime.rs#L35) and [`tokio/src/runtime/context/current.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context/current.rs#L33).

**Read Next**
- [`tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/runtime.rs#L174)
- [`tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/handle.rs#L197)
- [`tokio/src/runtime/context/current.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context/current.rs#L33)
- [`tokio/src/runtime/context/runtime.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/context/runtime.rs#L35)
- [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/current_thread/mod.rs#L203)
- [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/mod.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/mod.rs#L87)
- [`tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/handle.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/scheduler/multi_thread/handle.rs#L55)
- [`tokio/src/task/spawn.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/task/spawn.rs#L174)
- [`tokio-macros/src/entry.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio-macros/src/entry.rs#L582)

**Tests To Read Next**
- [`tokio/tests/rt_basic.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/tests/rt_basic.rs#L29) for “spawned work does not progress until `block_on` drives it”, plus `spawn_two`, `spawn_remote`, and shutdown behavior.
- [`tokio/tests/rt_threaded.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/tests/rt_threaded.rs#L27) for multi-thread scheduler behavior, task fanout, blocking, `block_in_place`, and shutdown.
- [`tokio/tests/rt_handle_block_on.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/tests/rt_handle_block_on.rs#L98) for `Handle::block_on` boundaries, including the current-thread caveat.
- [`tokio/tests/task_builder.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/tests/task_builder.rs#L7) for task naming and the `spawn`/`spawn_blocking`/`spawn_local` public surface.
- [`tokio/src/runtime/tests/task_combinations.rs`](C:/Users/samch/AppData/Local/Temp/samchon-graph-corpus/tokio/tokio/src/runtime/tests/task_combinations.rs#L209) for the runtime flavor matrix, including `LocalSet`.

I wrote the onboarding memories too; you can sanity-check them with `serena memories check` from the project root.
